text:p.text:span: Pipeline: Stage 1 chatbot â†’ Stage 2 approval â†’ Stage 3 MCP persistence
Cross-check against the assignment wording and current repository state
All stages are integrated in one pipeline: chat, approval, recording.
Stage 4 API endpoint runs the full state machine.
Graph nodes match the task: user interaction, admin approval, data recording.
Confirmed reservations trigger Stage 3 persistence only after approval.
README includes architecture, logic, setup, and deployment notes.
Stage 4 load test helper exists for runtime checks.
Load testing is present, but evidence is a helper module rather than full benchmark artifacts.
CI/CD and IaC are present at project level; Stage 4 folder now includes a working Terraform stack (main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, terraform.tfvars.example).
No GitHub / EPAM GitLab repository link is embedded inside the archive.
For coursework scope, Stage 4 is substantially implemented and aligned with the task.
For maximum score, the missing repo link and stronger Stage 4 deployment / CI artifacts should be added.
Presentation can proceed without inventing missing runtime screenshots.
Stage 4 is good enough to present as implemented. I would position it as DONE WITH MINOR GAPS, not as a perfect final delivery.
Unified LangGraph layer coordinates Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3 components
POST /stage4/run executes the graph with query, session, reservation input, and optional admin decision.
The graph uses conditional edges after user interaction and after admin approval.
Stage 4 orchestrates only; Stage 3 still owns the write path and persistence format.
Operational path implemented in the orchestrator
1) Receive query and session; optionally receive reservation_input and admin_decision.
2) user_interaction node calls Stage 1 router for chat requests.
3) If reservation_input exists, save_reservation(...) creates a pending reservation in SQLite.
4) Conditional edge checks whether both reservation_id and admin_decision are present.
5) admin_approval node updates reservation status to confirmed or rejected.
6) Second conditional edge sends only confirmed reservations to data_recording.
7) data_recording loads the reservation row and calls the Stage 3 MCP client.
8) Final state returns chatbot response, reservation status, recording flag, and error field.
Three code touchpoints define the Stage 4 solution

draw:custom-shape.draw:name: Text 7
draw:custom-shape.text:p: Defines Stage4State, graph nodes, and conditional routing. user_interaction handles chat + optional reservation creation. admin_approval applies confirmed/rejected decision. data_recording sends only confirmed reservations to Stage 3 MCP client.

draw:custom-shape.draw:name: Text 11
draw:custom-shape.text:p: FastAPI layer for Stage 4. GET /health checks service status. POST /stage4/run executes full pipeline with query, session, optional reservation_input, and optional admin_decision.

draw:custom-shape.draw:name: Text 15
draw:custom-shape.text:p: Load test helper with two modes: chat_only and confirm_flow. Returns summary metrics: total, successes, failures, avg_ms, p50_ms, p95_ms, max_ms.

text:p.text:span: Tests cover the graph behavior and API contract
test_stage4_orchestrator.py: 4 tests for confirmed flow, rejected flow, chat-only routing, and DB state update.
test_stage4_service.py: 2 tests for /health and /stage4/run response contract.
test_stage4_load_test.py: 2 tests for chat_only and confirm_flow summary behavior.
That gives at least 2 tests per Stage 4 module, matching the stated bonus requirement.
HTTP API health and run endpoint
The archive does not include repository URL evidence required in the answer field.
No runtime screenshots for Stage 4 execution were found inside the archive.
Load testing exists, but not as full benchmark documentation with component-by-component measured screenshots.
Present this as a working unified orchestration layer with automated validation, plus a short note on remaining packaging / evidence gaps.
What can be claimed now and what should still be added before final submission
Unified system exists: chatbot, approval, and MCP persistence are wired through LangGraph.
Stable basic pipeline behavior is covered by automated tests.
README and stage-specific documentation are present.
Stage 4 is presentation-ready in the same style as previous stages.
Repository link in the answer field or README.
Real runtime screenshots for Stage 4 execution and load checks.
Optionally add CI run evidence and terraform apply screenshots for Stage 4 deployment.

My assessment: Stage 4 is implemented well enough to continue with the presentation. It is not materially off-task; the remaining issues are packaging / evidence quality, not core orchestration logic.
â€œStage 4 unified orchestration is implemented and tested. Remaining improvements are repository-link packaging, richer runtime evidence, and stronger deployment automation artifacts.â€
